sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports feeling okay overall, and has experienced significant improvement since being vaccinated, allowing him to attend more social events and spend time with his grandchildren. He reports occasional nasal congestion. He denies chest pain, shortness of breath, abdominal pain, nausea, or vomiting. He states he's been consistently using the blood pressure cuff once daily and finds the readings normal. He is currently taking lisinopril as prescribed and reports taking it as directed. He's been attending therapy once a week, primarily virtually, and feels in-person sessions are helpful. He has a good support system at home with his husband and children nearby.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is 132/84 mmHg. Heart rate is 72 bpm. Carotid bruits are absent. Lung sounds are clear bilaterally. There is trace lower extremity edema bilaterally. EKG shows no acute abnormalities. The nurse's pre-visit EKG was normal.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's depression is currently well-managed. His blood pressure is controlled with current medication regimen. No acute cardiac abnormalities are identified.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Continue lisinopril 20 mg daily. Schedule a mammogram. Advise the patient to monitor blood pressure at home and contact the office if readings are consistently elevated. Arrange follow-up in 3 months to assess ongoing management of depression and blood pressure.
